meanings: to behave like a spoiled child
the way things are or should be
not very (this form only used as adverb)
1. one copy e.g. of a document
drink!(said repeatedly as a party cheer)
liar (sometimes said with not much seriousness)

(orig) charcoal brazier in a floor well
actions committed in a former life

not writing or contacting for a while
to be used to a job
to go to sleep (i.e. a limb)
doing a thing from beginning to end
arrival (in a country at work etc.)
to carry on back or shoulder
local express (train slower than an express)
mystery or detective genre (movie novel etc.)
by all means (with sense of not taking no for an answer)
search (esp. for someone or something missing)
to drop in for a short visit
public position or attitude (as opposed to private thoughts)
to arrive somewhere after a struggle
implying (negatively) that something is full of e.g. mistakes
to add one thing to another
to have no more at hand
(husband and wife) earning a living together
to act as an agent for
hall used for martial arts training
winning a prize or place (in a contest)
to move right ahead (with the work)
counter for nights of a stay
I am glad to meet you
something heard for the first time
to retire to a private place
eighth sign of Chinese zodiac (The Ram 1pm- 3pm south-southwest June)
not to work out (ie a deal)

to deprecate oneself and praise the listener
to come apart at the seams
day care worker in a kindergarten nursery school etc.
fourth sign of Chinese zodiac (The Hare 5am- 7am east February)
counter for flat objects (e.g. sheets of paper)

to take an extensive view of
private home providing lodging for travelers

good sense (for music style tact etc.)
down to coincide (fall on the same day)

word: デモンストレーション
reading: デモンストレーション

meanings: drill game under lights (e.g. baseball) (lit: nighter)

example.ja: この政策は大きな変化を齎した。
example.en: This policy brought about major changes.

example.ja: 星が瞬いている。
example.en: The stars are twinkling.

example.ja: 彼は自分の決定を翻した。
example.en: He reversed his decision.

example.ja: 他人を蔑むべきではない。
example.en: You should not look down on others.

example.ja: 失敗した人を嘲るのは卑劣だ。
example.en: It is cowardly to mock those who have failed.

example.ja: 恐怖で慄いた。
example.en: I trembled with fear.

example.ja: 人生は儚いものだ。
example.en: Life is a fleeting thing.

example.ja: 私は機械に疎い。
example.en: I am unfamiliar with machines.

example.ja: 上司に媚びるのは嫌いだ。
example.en: I dislike fawning on my superiors.

example.ja: 子供が拗ねてしまった。
example.en: The child started sulking.

example.ja: 母は子供を窘めた。
example.en: The mother gently admonished the child.

example.ja: 穴を塞いでください。
example.en: Please block the hole.

example.ja: 人の感情を弄んではいけない。
example.en: You must not toy with people's feelings.

example.ja: そう僻まないでください。
example.en: Please don't take it the wrong way.

example.ja: 汚染が環境を蝕んでいる。
example.en: Pollution is eroding the environment.

example.ja: 敵を侮ってはならない。
example.en: You must not underestimate the enemy.

example.ja: 彼の発言は揶揄に満ちていた。
example.en: His remarks were full of mockery.

example.ja: 職人としての矜持を持っている。
example.en: He has pride as a craftsman.

example.ja: 刹那の判断が命を救った。
example.en: A split-second decision saved a life.

example.ja: それは杞憂に過ぎなかった。
example.en: It was nothing but a groundless fear.

example.ja: 彼は逡巡の末に決断した。
example.en: He made a decision after much hesitation.

example.ja: 彼女は慟哭した。
example.en: She wailed bitterly.

example.ja: 旧友との邂逅に驚いた。
example.en: I was surprised by the chance meeting with an old friend.

example.ja: 忌憚のないご意見をお聞かせください。
example.en: Please give us your frank opinion.

example.ja: 組織が瓦解した。
example.en: The organization collapsed.

example.ja: 恣意的な判断は避けるべきだ。
example.en: Arbitrary decisions should be avoided.

example.ja: 怠惰な生活を送っている。
example.en: He is leading an indolent life.

example.ja: 知識に対して貪欲であるべきだ。
example.en: One should be greedy for knowledge.

example.ja: 彼は寡黙な人だ。
example.en: He is a taciturn person.

example.ja: 傲慢な態度は嫌われる。
example.en: An arrogant attitude is disliked.

example.ja: 狡猾な手段を使った。
example.en: He used cunning means.

example.ja: 辛辣な批評を受けた。
example.en: He received scathing criticism.

example.ja: 荘厳な雰囲気に包まれた。
example.en: It was enveloped in a solemn atmosphere.

example.ja: 合格の知らせに安堵した。
example.en: I felt relief at the news of passing.

example.ja: 惰性で続けているだけだ。
example.en: I am just continuing out of inertia.

example.ja: 心の中で葛藤が続いている。
example.en: An inner struggle continues in my heart.

example.ja: 安全性に対する懸念が高まっている。
example.en: Concerns about safety are growing.

example.ja: このシステムは脆弱だ。
example.en: This system is vulnerable.

example.ja: 潤沢な資金がある。
example.en: There are ample funds.

example.ja: 稀有な才能の持ち主だ。
example.en: He is a person of rare talent.

example.ja: 凄惨な事件が起きた。
example.en: A gruesome incident occurred.

example.ja: 都会の喧噪から離れたい。
example.en: I want to get away from the clamor of the city.

example.ja: 長期の戦争で国が疲弊した。
example.en: The country was exhausted by the long war.

example.ja: 躊躇なく行動した。
example.en: I acted without hesitation.
